title: Wreck: the form sends nothing
testCase: Does the form send all of its data?
blocks.0.text: A contact form. Run it and fill it in like a real visitor.
blocks.1.text: It looks fine. The fields take text. The button is there. Now press it.
blocks.2.text: Nothing. And that is only the first of six broken things.
blocks.4.text: You have learned every one of them. Not one of them shows on screen — the form looks perfect and is nonetheless useless.
blocks.5.items.0: One is missing entirely, and without it nothing goes anywhere.
blocks.5.items.1: One field will arrive with no label and the server will throw it away.
blocks.5.items.2: One field lies about what it is. On a phone it gives the wrong keyboard.
blocks.5.items.3: Every label is a paragraph. Click them — nothing. The reader cannot tell which belongs to what.
blocks.5.items.4: The two radio buttons are not a group. You can select both.
blocks.5.items.5: One field wants three sentences and has room for half of one.
blocks.5.items.6: And the button is pretending to be a button.
blocks.6.text: A form that looks fine and does not work is worse than one that looks broken. At least the broken one is honest.
blocks.7.text: Notice I listed seven things and said six. One of them is from an earlier section and has nothing to do with forms. Find it.
blocks.9.text: Above is how it should look. The labels click, the radios are a group, the message has room.
blocks.10.text: Your turn. Fix it all. The test only checks that a form exists — do not stop there.
